reasoning: The sink at Helpers.php line 1130 is real: $class and $placement are concatenated into HTML attribute values without esc_attr(). However, no attacker-controlled path to this sink exists in the current codebase. (1) $class: every external caller goes through createQuestionMark() or createOverrideTooltip(), both of which hardcode $class to fixed CSS class string literals ('dashicons dashicons-editor-help dgwt-wcas-questio-mark' and 'dashicons dashicons-lock dgwt-wcas-override-tooltip'). No caller exposes $class to external input. (2) $placement: every call site passes only $id and $content arguments; $placement always defaults to the literal 'right' and is never supplied by any caller. (3) Context: the output is rendered exclusively in WordPress admin settings pages, gated by admin_init and current_user_can('manage_options'/'manage_woocommerce'). Admins are trusted principals in the WordPress security model. (4) No apply_filters or do_action hooks expose $placement or $class through createTooltip(). The finding is a theoretical future-caller risk (a code-quality issue), not an exploitable vulnerability given the current codebase. Verdict: NOT REAL as an exploitable vulnerability.
